images.altText: Five men and one woman pose together in an ornate meeting room in the State Department.

articles.title: Lebanon, Israel agree to direct negotiations after Washington talks

children.content: Israel and Lebanon agree to direct negotiations following talks in Washington hailed by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio as a "historic opportunity" for peace.

image.altText: A boy is comforted by a woman, in a group of people all dressed in black.

children.content: The talks come as both the US and Iran continue to impose their own separate restrictions on the flow of vessels in and out of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: Trump says US-Iran talks could restart within days, Israel-Lebanon meeting 'productive'

updates.title: Vance says Trump wants 'grand bargain' with Iran
Several oil tankers stopped in a row in Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Peace talks, strait closed, China calls out US blockade: What to know today

children.content: One week into the ceasefire, two sets of peace talks have taken place but the only commitment is to talk more.

image.altText: ABC reporter Matthew Doran standing in front of the Riyadh skyline.

items.title: 'Potential is significant': The untapped fuel source already on our shores

children.content: Australia controls less than 20 per cent of our liquid fuel supply and relies on imports for the other 80-plus per cent we need to keep the nation moving. But proponents of biofuels argue it does not need to be this way.

image.altText: Yellow sign states "pump not in use" is attached to the petrol bowser.

items.title: Your brain explained: Why it's getting harder to pay attention

children.content: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

image.altText: An illustration of an abstract human brain, using coloured paper cut-outs against a blue paper background.

items.title: Reality star left fearing death after fake weight loss jab

children.content: Aisleyne Horgan-Wallace lay on her bed, wincing in pain, convinced she was about to die after injecting herself with a cheap, unregulated weight loss drug.

image.altText: Aisleyne Horgan-Wallace sits in a lounge room, on a pink couch with flowers behind her

items.title: What a 'super El Niño' would mean for Australia's weather in 2026

children.content: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly thanks to the probable emergence of an incoming El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires are on the way.

articles.title: Flood watches issued as former Cyclone Maila brings days of heavy rain

children.content: Minor flood watches have been issued for several Far North Queensland catchments as former Tropical Cyclone Maila delivers sustained heavy rainfall, with forecasters warning transport routes could be disrupted.

items.title: PM in Brunei as Chalmers warns of 'dangerous' time for economy

children.content: Anthony Albanese will leverage Australia's food exports while in Brunei in a bid to shore up long-term fuel supplies. Meanwhile, Treasurer Jim Chalmers is off to Washington following the release of the IMF's latest global economic outlook. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese presser

items.title: IMF warns 'swift tightening' of interest rates may be needed to slow inflation

children.content: The International Monetary Fund paints three economic outlook scenarios that each get progressively worse depending on how long Middle East energy supplies are disrupted.

image.altText: A man walks along a rock wall with ocean and ships in the background.

items.title: Lone backpacker remains on outback station as fuel crisis sees jobs dry up

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

gallery.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

items.title: Trump lashes out at European ally Meloni amid tensions over pope and NATO

children.content: The US president says Giorgia Meloni "does not care" about Iran getting a nuclear weapon, after the Italian PM defended the pope and cancelled a military deal with Israel.

image.altText: Giorgia Meloni and Donald Trump shaking hands.

items.title: ASX to follow Wall Street higher, oil prices sink on hope of further US-Iran peace talks

children.content: The Australian share market is likely to rise in morning trade as global investors bet on the US-Iran war being over within months, despite fuel shortages, higher inflation and a possible economic slowdown. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX board with stock names and prices blurred as board moves

items.title: Overcrowded boat sinks in rough seas with 250 people on board

children.content: The UN says the boat sank with about 250 people on board, including children, in the Andaman Sea "due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding".

image.altText: BANGLADESH COAST GUARD

items.title: FIFA provides more expensive World Cup tickets after backlash over prices

children.content: After a backlash over ticket prices, FIFA puts up a new option for fans — and it is even more expensive.

items.title: Leaving your laptop on for the commute? Here's the impact

children.content: While modern laptops are good at saving battery life and keeping cool, extended periods in sleep mode could still be putting a strain on them over time.

items.title: How The Babadook inspired the 'scariest movie you'll ever hear'

children.content: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

children.content: What are the pyramids made out of? And which Caribbean island doesn't appear in the Beach Boys song Kokomo?

children.content: A NSW hospital becomes the first in Australia to offer a new stroke-prevention treatment that has been available in the United States for a decade.

items.title: Murdered farmer's daughter speaks out as police investigate violent death

children.content: The daughter of a murdered Ouyen farmer says her father had recently made reports to police about sheep thefts from his farm.

items.title: Hidden cameras in change rooms cause regional town hall to postpone events

children.content: An investigation into hidden cameras in change rooms has forced the Horsham Town Hall to postpone events in western Victoria.

items.title: Brothers living in squalor missed multiple health appointments, report finds

children.content: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.

items.title: Remote council jobs a priority as NT Aboriginal affairs plan launched

children.content: The Northern Territory government has released its Aboriginal Affairs framework as the territory goes backwards on several Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: Steve Edgington wears a suit and tie while standing outside NT Parliament.

items.title: 'She was extraordinary': Famed sculptor Pat Moseley remembered

children.content: Across dusty outback tracks to the promenades of Adelaide, sculptor Pat Moseley turned limestone and bronze into the faces of legends.

items.title: Four-year-old Leo's rare condition a 'ticking time bomb' but there is hope

children.content: Four-year-old Leo has a rare form of epilepsy, and his family has turned to creative ways to fund help for him. An advocacy group says others are in the same boat, and it hopes a new inquiry will help.

image.altText: A woman in a green dress with red hair smiling at a young brunette boy on her lap smiling at the camera.

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan kick off Australia visit with stop at Melbourne hospital

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have begun their multi-day visit to Australia in Melbourne, meeting with patients at a children's hospital.

image.altText: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle

items.title: Gina Rinehart judgment looming in high-stakes battle over mining riches

children.content: Gina Rinehart, the wealthiest person in Australia, expects a judgment to be handed down today in an explosive court battle with the family of her father's former business partner over iron ore royalties.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.

items.title: Coalition's migration proposal faces practical questions

children.content: The Coalition's new migration policy aims to increase screening of visa applicants and "boot out" people who do not abide by Australia's values, but questions remain about how the plan would work in practice.

image.altText: Angus Taylor at a press conference in Parliament House.

items.title: Nurse highlights WA outback through social media

children.content: Amid chronic nursing shortages in Western Australia's regions, one nurse highlights the profession's unique benefits and captures his experience on social media.

items.title: Student warns against ‘shying away’ from AI in schools

children.content: Thousands of students are using AI to transform the way they learn, but many remain concerned about what the technology means for the environment, jobs and creativity.

children.content: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

items.title: NFL reporter resigns days after photos with Patriots coach published

items.title: Usyk calls kickboxer Verhoeven a dangerous opponent ahead of unlikely title fight

children.content: World heavyweight boxing champion Oleksandr Usyk says it is time to do what he wants ahead of his title bout against a kickboxer who has only had one boxing fight.
